basics.label: Experienced software engineer skilled in front-end and back-end technologies. Proficient in software design principles, design patterns, and clean architectures.
basics.name: Yuniel Acosta
basics.summary:
I build backend systems that have to be right the first time — Kafka pipelines moving millions of events a day in banking and fintech, where a silent failure costs more than a slow sprint.

My core conviction: software that's hard to change was designed wrong from day one. Hexagonal Architecture and Domain-Driven Design aren't conference buzzwords to me — they're how I keep business logic isolated from infrastructure noise, so a database swap or a new integration never turns into a six-month migration.

The same logic drives how I verify that logic. TDD gives me a tight feedback loop while I write the code. BDD keeps the conversation with the business anchored to real scenarios instead of assumptions. Spec-Driven Development — writing the spec before a line of implementation, often with an AI agent doing the first pass — is now the backbone of how I scope complex integrations.

Stack: Kotlin or Java and the JVM for production backend systems. Spring Boot when its conventions earn their keep. Apache Kafka as the spine of event-driven architectures built to scale without falling over under load. On the frontend, I reach for Astro and Vue.js — islands architecture and reactivity, without the framework weight a page didn't ask for.

I've spent years inside high-stakes environments: banking core systems, fraud detection processing millions of daily authentication events, fintech platforms where downtime is a regulatory incident, not an inconvenience. That's where I learned "good enough" architecture fails exactly when you can least afford it.

More recently, I've built my own AI engineering harness on top of OpenCode — custom agents, subagents, and prompt libraries that absorb the scaffolding so I can focus on decisions that actually need a human brain. I think this is closer to how most engineers will work within a couple of years, not a novelty bolted onto a normal workflow.

Outside client work, I build in public: distributed systems, Kafka, Rust, and observability content on my blog and YouTube channel. Current side projects: Corvus, an autonomous AI agent framework in Kotlin and Rust for long-running orchestration, and Profile Tailors, a multi-channel content distribution SaaS on Spring Boot 4, Kotlin, and WebFlux.

Not chasing the next framework hype cycle, or a team that confuses moving fast with moving anywhere. Looking for hard problems, systems worth designing properly, and teams that still care about the craft.

references.name: Mayret Lodos
references.reference:
I have had the privilege of knowing Yuniel and have witnessed firsthand his tenacity, commitment, and passion for software development. He stands out for his leadership skills, demonstrated in the way he makes sound technical decisions, maintains balance within the team, and integrates different perspectives to deliver the best possible outcomes.

His adaptability to evolving technologies and his ability to design effective solutions make him a highly reliable software professional. Moreover, his enthusiasm for development goes beyond the technical dimension: he breathes and bleeds programming, approaching each project with dedication and excellence, showing clearly that for him it is not just a profession, but a true vocation.

references.name: Yuniesky Martinez Espinosa
references.reference:
Focused, intelligent, and driven by constant curiosity, Yuniel is a senior full stack developer with strong expertise in his field and a broad understanding of complementary technologies and disciplines. He quickly integrates new knowledge and applies it effectively to build scalable, high-quality solutions.

His ability to combine deep technical expertise with a cross-disciplinary perspective allows him to approach complex projects holistically, ensuring robust architecture, maintainable code, and an efficiency-oriented mindset. His sense of responsibility and professional rigor consistently deliver results of exceptional value.

highlights: Leading migration of ESB integrations (TIBCO) to Apache Camel and Kafka pipelines within BBVA's NOVA framework — rethinking data flow across a distributed architecture at real scale.
Previously at Deutsche Bank CLM: built Spring Boot and Kafka services establishing architecture patterns the team adopted as standard.
Design and implement microservices following Clean Architecture and DDD principles.
Translate regulatory requirements into concrete technical specifications.
Lead workshops on event-driven architecture and Kafka patterns.
Mentor junior developers on SOLID principles and TDD.
Drive CI/CD adoption across teams with complex delivery cycles.

Build and maintain a custom AI engineering harness — prompt libraries, autonomous agents, subagents, MCPs, and Spec-Driven Development (SDD) workflows using OpenCode — to accelerate delivery and reduce cognitive overhead on complex integrations.
Reduced average feature delivery time by 30% through improved estimation, sprint planning, and AI-assisted spec generation.
Established coding standards adopted by 4 project teams across two banking clients.
Delivered 6 regulatory compliance features ahead of deadline, avoiding potential audit findings.
Cut boilerplate scaffolding time by ~60% by introducing SDD workflows and AI agent orchestration into the team's development cycle.
work.name: GFT Technologies
work.summary: I work on banking transformation projects for top-tier European financial institutions — currently BBVA, previously Deutsche Bank CLM. At BBVA I'm leading the migration of ESB integrations (TIBCO) to Apache Camel and Kafka pipelines within the NOVA framework. It's not just a technology swap — it means rethinking how data flows across a distributed architecture at real scale.

highlights: Led migration of core services from Spring Boot 2.7 to Spring Boot 3, reducing runtime dependencies by 40% and improving startup time by 3x.
Designed and implemented a new event-driven integration service from scratch using Kafka, consolidating data from 12+ external sources and enabling instant client data pre-fill during onboarding.
Reduced StreamLine user onboarding time by optimizing data validation workflows and automating compliance checks.
Modernized 4 microservices maintaining 99.9% uptime during all migration phases.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams (Compliance, Legal, Frontend) to deliver regulatory-compliant features on time.
work.name: Deutsche Bank
work.summary: Senior Software Engineer in the Client Lifecycle Management (CLM) team, contributing to the bank's Know Your Client onboarding platform serving Investment Banking clients globally. Focused on service modernization, event-driven integrations, and regulatory compliance delivery.

highlights: Built internal APIs for malicious behavior detection across MercadoLibre.com and MercadoPago.com login and registration flows — systems processing 3M+ daily authentication events.
Designed and implemented risk policies and scoring systems to identify and block fraudulent account activity in real time.
Reduced fraudulent account registrations by 22% through new scoring heuristics.
Improved detection latency by 40% after API refactoring and caching layer optimization.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to deliver scalable backend solutions supporting high-traffic retail and payments operations.
Conducted performance and reliability testing to minimize downtime and improve user experience at scale.
work.name: Mercado Libre
work.position: Software Engineer | Kotlin & Java Backend Developer
work.summary: Part of the Trust & Safety team, building the systems that protect login and registration flows for both MercadoLibre.com and MercadoPago.com across millions of daily transactions in the retail and payments ecosystem.

highlights: Reduced data synchronization time between platform APIs and MMC from ~40 minutes to ~6 minutes, significantly cutting processing time and operational costs.
Improved code quality by introducing unit and integration tests and promoting SOLID principles across the codebase.
Designed and implemented a centralized configuration pattern for all platform microservices, simplifying deployments and configuration management.
Implemented a GitLab CI continuous integration pipeline enabling fast, reliable delivery and reducing deployment errors.
Maintained and optimized high-traffic APIs ensuring availability and performance across multiple retail market integrations.
